items.title: Overview

items.title: FastAPI
items.text: Provides API endpoints.

items.title: OpenAI Client
items.text: Handles communication with OpenAI.

items.title: Docker
items.text: Provides a reproducible environment.

items.title: Run Unit Tests
items.text: docker compose run --rm app pytest

items.title: Run Coverage
items.text:
docker compose run --rm app \
  pytest --cov=app

items.title: Expected Result
items.text:
All tests pass.
Coverage exceeds 80%.

items.title: Docker Setup
items.text:
Build:

```bash docker compose build

items.title: Quick Diagnostic Checklist
items.text:
From your project root, run:

pwd
ls -la .devcontainer
cat .devcontainer/devcontainer.json
docker ps
docker context ls
docker compose config

These are the exact diagnostics recommended in the Dev Container troubleshooting guide.

items.title: Verify devcontainer.json
items.text:
Your documented working example uses:

{
  "name": "Docker Python Template",
  "dockerComposeFile": "../docker-compose.yml",
  "service": "app",
  "workspaceFolder": "/app",
  "shutdownAction": "stopCompose"
}

Key checks: - dockerComposeFile points to the correct file - service matches the service name in docker-compose.yml - workspaceFolder matches the mounted path (/app)

items.title: Validate Docker Compose
items.text:
Run:

docker compose config

If this fails, VS Code Dev Containers will also fail. Validate compose before reopening the container.

items.title: Check the Container Stays Running
items.text:
If VS Code builds the container but cannot attach, run:

docker compose ps
docker compose logs app --tail=100

A container that exits immediately prevents VS Code from attaching. A common development workaround is to use a long-running command such as:

command: sleep infinity

items.title: Rebuild the Dev Container
items.text:
Use the VS Code command palette:

Cmd+Shift+P
Dev Containers: Rebuild Container

or:

Dev Containers: Rebuild and Reopen in Container

These are the recommended recovery actions after fixing configuration problems.

items.title: Reset Everything
items.text:
For this project, the recommended reset sequence is:

docker compose down
docker compose build --no-cache
docker compose up

Then reopen the Dev Container.

items.title: Verify the Environment After Attachment
items.text:
Once attached, confirm the runtime environment:

pwd
which python
python --version
pip list | grep pytest

Confirm: - Workspace is /app - Python is inside the container - pytest is installed

items.title: Cause
items.text: This warning appears when Git finds files in the working tree that have changes but are not staged for commit.

items.title: Resolution
items.text:
1. Review changed files:

```bash git status 

2. Stage files you want to keep:

   ```bash
git add <file>

or stage all changes:

```bash git add . 

3. If you do not want to keep the changes, discard them:

   ```bash
git restore <file>

or discard all unstaged changes:

```bash git restore . 

4. Commit staged changes:

   ```bash
git commit -m "Your commit message"

items.title: Notes
items.text:
• If the warning appears during a scripted operation, make sure the script is running in the correct repository and that uncommitted changes are intended.
  • Use git diff to inspect unstaged modifications before staging or discarding them.
